metadata.description: Challenges validating platform-specific test coverage including desktop tests for SSH, SMB, and FTP clients, Wasm protocol stubs throwing NotImplementedError, iOS stubs with proper documentation and Result types, platform factory tests, expect/actual declaration completeness, and platform-specific source set organization

challenges.name: Desktop SSH Client Test Coverage
challenges.description: Verify that desktop-specific SFTP/SSH client tests exist in desktopTest source set. Tests must cover connection establishment, authentication (password and key-based), file upload/download, directory listing, path traversal prevention, connection timeout handling, and session cleanup. At least 15 test methods must exist for SSH client functionality.

outputs.name: ssh_test_files
outputs.description: List of test files covering SSH/SFTP functionality in desktopTest

outputs.name: test_method_count
outputs.description: Number of SSH-related test methods found

outputs.name: coverage_gaps
outputs.description: Required SSH test scenarios that are not covered

assertions.message: At least one desktop test file must cover SSH/SFTP client functionality
At least 15 SSH client test methods must exist in desktopTest
All required SSH test scenarios must be covered

challenges.name: Desktop SMB Client Test Coverage
challenges.description: Verify that desktop-specific SMB client tests exist in desktopTest source set. Tests must cover share connection, NTLM and Kerberos authentication, file CRUD operations, directory traversal, connection guards on deleteFile and getFileInfo, large file handling, and reconnection behavior. At least 12 test methods must exist.

outputs.name: smb_test_files
outputs.description: List of test files covering SMB functionality in desktopTest

outputs.name: test_method_count
outputs.description: Number of SMB-related test methods found

outputs.name: coverage_gaps
outputs.description: Required SMB test scenarios that are not covered

assertions.message: At least one desktop test file must cover SMB client functionality
At least 12 SMB client test methods must exist in desktopTest
All required SMB test scenarios must be covered

challenges.name: Desktop FTP Client Test Coverage
challenges.description: Verify that desktop-specific FTP client tests exist in desktopTest source set. Tests must cover active and passive mode connections, anonymous and authenticated access, file transfer (binary and ASCII), directory navigation, FTP command injection prevention, timeout handling, and TLS/FTPS negotiation. At least 12 test methods must exist.

outputs.name: ftp_test_files
outputs.description: List of test files covering FTP functionality in desktopTest

outputs.name: test_method_count
outputs.description: Number of FTP-related test methods found

outputs.name: coverage_gaps
outputs.description: Required FTP test scenarios that are not covered

assertions.message: At least one desktop test file must cover FTP client functionality
At least 12 FTP client test methods must exist in desktopTest
All required FTP test scenarios must be covered

challenges.name: Wasm Protocol Stubs Throw NotImplementedError
challenges.description: Verify that all protocol service implementations in wasmJsMain throw NotImplementedError (or UnsupportedOperationException) with descriptive messages. Wasm does not support native socket connections, so FTP, SFTP, SMB, WebDAV, and S3 stubs must fail explicitly rather than silently. Each stub method must include the protocol name and operation in its error message.

outputs.name: stub_results
outputs.description: Map of protocol to list of stub methods and their error behavior

outputs.name: silent_stubs
outputs.description: Stub methods that silently succeed instead of throwing

outputs.name: missing_message_stubs
outputs.description: Stubs that throw without descriptive error messages

outputs.name: protocols_checked
outputs.description: Number of protocol stubs verified

assertions.message: No Wasm protocol stub may silently succeed instead of throwing
All Wasm stubs must include descriptive error messages with protocol and operation names
At least 5 protocol stubs must be verified (FTP, SFTP, SMB, WebDAV, S3)

challenges.name: Wasm Protocol Stub Test Coverage
challenges.description: Verify that wasmJsTest source set contains tests that invoke each protocol stub and assert the expected NotImplementedError is thrown. Each protocol must have at least one test verifying the stub behavior. Tests must verify both the exception type and the error message content.

outputs.name: stub_test_files
outputs.description: Test files in wasmJsTest that verify protocol stubs

outputs.name: test_method_count
outputs.description: Number of stub verification test methods

outputs.name: untested_protocols
outputs.description: Protocols whose stubs are not tested in wasmJsTest

assertions.message: At least one wasmJsTest file must test protocol stub behavior
All protocol stubs must have corresponding tests in wasmJsTest
At least 5 stub verification tests must exist

challenges.name: iOS Stubs Documented with KDoc and TODOs
challenges.description: Verify that all iOS platform stubs in iosMain include KDoc documentation explaining the expected iOS implementation, Result return types where applicable, and TODO comments referencing specific iOS frameworks (e.g., Network.framework for SFTP, FileProvider for cloud storage). Each stub file must have at least one KDoc block and at least one TODO comment.

outputs.name: documentation_results
outputs.description: Map of iOS stub file to documentation coverage metrics

outputs.name: undocumented_stubs
outputs.description: iOS stub files missing KDoc documentation

outputs.name: missing_todos
outputs.description: iOS stub files missing TODO comments for implementation guidance

outputs.name: result_types_used
outputs.description: Whether Result return types are used in iOS stubs

assertions.message: All iOS stub files must include KDoc documentation
All iOS stub files must include TODO comments referencing target iOS frameworks
iOS stubs must use Result return types where applicable

challenges.name: iOS Stubs Use Result Return Types
challenges.description: Verify that iOS platform stubs return Result<T> types instead of throwing exceptions or returning null for operations that may fail. Check all actual fun declarations in iosMain for network operations, authentication, and file I/O. Each must return Result<T> or a sealed class that models success/failure.

outputs.name: return_type_results
outputs.description: Map of function signature to its return type analysis

outputs.name: non_result_functions
outputs.description: Functions that return raw types instead of Result<T>

outputs.name: functions_checked
outputs.description: Number of actual fun declarations analyzed

assertions.message: All iOS stub functions for failable operations must return Result<T>
At least 5 actual fun declarations must be analyzed in iosMain
All return type analyses must be valid

challenges.name: Platform Factory Test Presence
challenges.description: Verify that platform-specific factory functions (createHttpClient, createSecureStorage, createPlatformFileSystem, etc.) have tests in each platform's test source set. Each expect/actual pair must have at least one test verifying the factory produces a valid, functional instance. Check desktopTest, androidTest (androidUnitTest), wasmJsTest, and iosTest.

outputs.name: factory_test_results
outputs.description: Map of platform to list of factory functions and their test status

outputs.name: untested_factories
outputs.description: Factory functions without tests on any platform

outputs.name: platforms_with_tests
outputs.description: Number of platforms that have factory tests

assertions.message: All platform factory functions must have tests on at least one platform
At least 2 platforms must have factory function tests
All factory test status results must be valid

challenges.name: Expect/Actual Declaration Completeness
challenges.description: Verify that every expect declaration in commonMain has a corresponding actual declaration in all four platform source sets (androidMain, desktopMain, iosMain, wasmJsMain). Scan for expect fun, expect class, and expect object declarations and verify each has matching actual declarations. Report any missing actual implementations per platform.

outputs.name: expect_actual_map
outputs.description: Map of expect declarations to their actual implementations per platform

outputs.name: missing_actuals
outputs.description: Expect declarations missing actual implementations on one or more platforms

outputs.name: expect_declarations_found
outputs.description: Total number of expect declarations found in commonMain

assertions.message: Every expect declaration must have matching actual declarations on all platforms
At least 5 expect declarations must be found in commonMain
All expect/actual mappings must be valid

challenges.name: Platform Source Set Organization
challenges.description: Verify that platform-specific source sets follow the expected directory structure. Each platform (android, desktop, ios, wasmJs) must have Main and Test directories. Platform-specific code must only exist in the correct source set (e.g., no Android-only imports in desktopMain). Verify no commonMain file imports platform-specific APIs directly.

outputs.name: structure_results
outputs.description: Verification of expected directory structure per platform

outputs.name: misplaced_imports
outputs.description: Platform-specific imports found in wrong source sets

outputs.name: common_platform_leaks
outputs.description: CommonMain files that directly import platform-specific APIs

outputs.name: source_sets_verified
outputs.description: Number of source sets verified

assertions.message: No platform-specific imports may exist in wrong source sets
No commonMain file may directly import platform-specific APIs
At least 8 source sets must be verified (4 Main + 4 Test)

challenges.name: Desktop Test Suite Minimum Count
challenges.description: Verify that the desktopTest source set plus commonTest (which runs on desktop) together provide at least 6,695 passing tests. This is the established baseline from the last verification. The test count must not decrease, only increase. Run the full desktop test suite and verify the pass count meets or exceeds the baseline.

outputs.name: test_results
outputs.description: Desktop test suite execution results

outputs.name: passing_count
outputs.description: Number of passing tests

outputs.name: failing_count
outputs.description: Number of failing tests

outputs.name: baseline_met
outputs.description: Whether the minimum test count baseline was met

assertions.message: Desktop test count must meet or exceed the 6,695 baseline
No desktop tests may be failing
The minimum test count baseline must be met
